items.title: Amateur armed with ChatGPT solves an Erdős problem
items.author: pr337h4m
items.summary: An amateur used an AI system built by Amazon to solve a long-standing Erdős problem. This achievement highlights AI's potential in advancing mathematical research. The solution was verified by prominent mathematicians.

items.title: USB Cheat Sheet (2022)
items.author: gwerbret
items.summary: USB supports data modes from USB1.1 to USB4 v2, with speeds ranging from 7.5W to 240W. It includes various connectors and modes, with clear distinctions between them. USB is fully encrypted for secure payments.

items.title: GnuPG – post-quantum crypto landing in mainline
items.author: zdkaster
items.summary: GnuPG 2.5 series introduced Kyber as a post-quantum encryption algorithm, while the 2.4 series is ending soon. Post-quantum keys are expected to be significantly larger, posing potential challenges for infrastructure.

items.title: OpenAI Privacy Filter
items.author: tanelpoder
items.summary: The OpenAI Privacy Filter is a model for detecting and redacting personally identifiable information in text. It is available under the Apache 2.0 license on Hugging Face and GitHub. It has limitations in detecting uncommon names and over-redacting public entities.

items.title: 1-Bit Hokusai's "The Great Wave" (2023)
items.author: stephen-hill
items.summary: An artist recreated Hokusai's "The Great Wave" as 1-bit pixel art using vintage Macintosh computers. The project aimed to render all 36 views of Mount Fuji in the original 512 x 342 pixel resolution. The work combines nostalgia for early Macintosh aesthetics with Japanese woodcut traditions.

items.title: Per-image PCA characterization of the Kodak image suite (PDF and JSON)
items.author: PearsonZero
items.summary: Per-image PCA of Kodak images reveals unique spectral characteristics for each image. Eigenvalue spectrum and eigenvector loadings differ across the 24 images. This analysis highlights deliberate curation in the image set.

items.title: America's Geothermal Breakthrough
items.author: sleepyguy
items.summary: In 2025, North America's geothermal sector saw an 85% increase in investment, unlocking 500 GW of potential. This surge followed significant technological advancements. By 2026, geothermal energy is gaining recognition for its efficiency and sustainability.

items.title: My Homemade PBX (2002)
items.author: rickcarlino
items.summary: A homemade PBX from 2002 involved building a dial telephone system using diode clamping, transformers, and DTMF technology for dialing and ringing. It utilized relays and a ring detector to manage line connections. The system could generate ringing voltages up to 90 V at 20 Hz.

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 on Day 0: From Fast Inference to Verified RL with SGLang and Miles
items.author: mji
items.summary: DeepSeek-V4 launched with full-parameter reinforcement learning support and efficient inference using SGLang and Miles. Day 0 included verified stability and broad hardware support.

items.title: New 10 GbE USB adapters are cooler, smaller, cheaper
items.author: calcifer
items.summary: New 10 GbE USB adapters are more efficient, compact, and affordable. They perform best on systems with USB 3.2 Gen 2x2 ports. Thunderbolt adapters remain superior for those needing full 10 Gbps speed.

items.title: Math Is Hard – OpenBSD Stories
items.author: signa11
items.summary: "Math Is Hard" is a series of stories about OpenBSD on Motorola 88K by Miod Vallat. It includes a 9-part narrative and a single-page version. The stories were shared on Hacker News, X, and Reddit.

items.title: Hokusai and Tesselations
items.author: srean
items.summary: Hokusai's work includes tessellations inspired by his famous wave, integrating geometric patterns with traditional Japanese art. These tessellations visually represent cryptocurrency market data. Hokusai's tessellations blend modern technology with classical art forms.

items.title: The George Business, by Roger Zelazny (1980)
items.author: xeonmc
items.summary: "The George Business" is a short story by Roger Zelazny, published in 1980, part of his Chronicles of Amber series. It is a standalone piece fitting within the fantasy genre.

items.title: GPT‑5.5 Bio Bug Bounty
items.author: Murfalo
items.summary: OpenAI launched a Bio Bug Bounty program for GPT-5.5, offering $25,000 for bypassing its biological safety measures. This program aims to identify potential vulnerabilities in AI safety. The initiative is part of broader efforts to enhance AI security.

items.title: Colorado Adds Open-Source Exemption to Age-Verification Bill
items.author: terminalbraid
items.summary: Colorado's age-verification bill now includes an exemption for open-source software. This change protects Linux and GitHub projects from compliance requirements. The exemption aims to balance privacy and regulation.

items.title: Plain text has been around for decades and it’s here to stay
items.author: rbanffy
items.summary: Plain text remains relevant due to its simplicity and utility in modern web and coding environments, with tools like Mockdown and Wiretext continuing its legacy. ASCII diagramming persists as a constrained, efficient method for communication and AI integration. Its enduring presence highlights its adaptability to new technologies.

items.title: Which one is more important: more parameters or more computation? (2021)
items.author: jxmorris12
items.summary: More computation often allows for better performance in deep learning models, but increasing model size can also improve results without additional computation. The optimal balance depends on the specific task and resource constraints.

items.title: Her life savings mysteriously disappeared after a systems glitch
items.author: danso
items.summary: A woman's life savings vanished due to a systems glitch at Fidelity Investments. The issue was reported in The New York Times. The company is investigating the incident.

items.title: Insights into firewood use by early Middle Pleistocene hominins
items.author: wslh
items.summary: Early Middle Pleistocene hominins at Lake Hula collected driftwood, including ash, willow, olive, pistachio, and pomegranate for fire, primarily based on availability. Charcoal analysis at Gesher Benot Ya'aqov shows they used any available wood for firewood. This indicates a flexible approach to firewood selection.

items.title: Only one side will be the true successor to MS-DOS – Windows 2.x
items.author: keepamovin
items.summary: Windows 2.x was intended to replace MS-DOS, offering a graphical user interface. It was released in 1987 and had features like overlapping and resizing windows. It was not fully successful as a successor to MS-DOS.
